q.zh: “特奥蒂瓦坎”这个名字是什么意思？

options.en: 'The place where the gods were created'

explain.en: The Aztec people 'named them Teotihuacán, which means the place where the gods were created' when they found the ruins in 1320.
explain.zh: 1320年阿兹特克人发现废墟时将其命名为特奥蒂瓦坎，意为“诸神被创造的地方”。

q.en: At its peak, about how many people lived in Teotihuacán?
q.zh: 鼎盛时期特奥蒂瓦坎约有多少人口？

explain.en: 'With a population of one to two hundred thousand people, it was the largest city in the Americas.'
explain.zh: “人口达10万到20万，是美洲最大的城市。”

q.en: Which is the largest structure in the ancient city?
q.zh: 古城中最大的建筑是哪一座？

options.zh: 奎策尔帕帕洛特尔宫殿

explain.en: 'the Pyramid of the Sun is the largest structure in the ancient city and the third-largest ancient pyramid in the world.'
explain.zh: “太阳金字塔是古城中最大的建筑，也是世界第三大古代金字塔。”

q.en: In what year was Teotihuacán named a UNESCO World Heritage Site?
q.zh: 特奥蒂瓦坎在哪一年被命名为联合国教科文组织世界遗产？

explain.en: 'In 1987, Teotihuacán was designated a UNESCO World Heritage Site.'
explain.zh: “1987年，特奥蒂瓦坎被指定为联合国教科文组织世界遗产。”

q.en: In the glossary, what does 'oriented' mean in the context of the pyramids?
q.zh: 词汇表中“oriented”（定位）在金字塔语境中是什么意思？

options.en: Positioned in relation to a direction or landmark
options.zh: 相对于某个方向或地标而定位

explain.en: The Glossary defines 'oriented (v.)' as 'positioned in relation to something else, such as a compass direction or landmark' (p. 7).
explain.zh: 词汇表将“oriented（动词）”定义为“相对于其他事物（如罗盘方向或地标）的位置”（第7页）。

q.en: The author repeatedly says 'we do not know' the builders' name, language, or rulers. What does this achieve?
q.zh: 作者反复说“我们不知道”建造者的名字、语言或统治者，这起到了什么作用？

options.en: It builds curiosity and shows that archaeology at the site is still ongoing
options.zh: 引发好奇，并表明遗址考古仍在进行

options.en: It proves the city was never real
options.zh: 证明这座城市从未真实存在

options.en: It shows the Aztecs built everything
options.zh: 表明一切都是阿兹特克人建造的

explain.en: 'we do not know what the people called themselves... what language they spoke... or the names of any of their rulers' keeps readers engaged with the unsolved puzzle.
explain.zh: “我们不知道这些人如何自称……说什么语言……或任何统治者的名字”，让读者持续被未解之谜吸引。

q.en: The book says builders changed the course of the San Juan River. Why?
q.zh: 书中说建造者改道圣胡安河，为什么？

options.en: To make it flow across the Avenue of the Dead at a right angle to fit the city grid
options.zh: 让它以直角穿过亡灵大道，以符合城市网格

options.en: To create a waterfall for tourists
To fill the pyramids with water

explain.en: Builders 'changed the course of the San Juan River to flow across the Avenue of the Dead at a right angle to conform to the grid.'
explain.zh: 建造者“改道圣胡安河，使其以直角穿过亡灵大道，以符合网格布局”。

q.en: What problems helped cause Teotihuacán's decline around 550 CE?
q.zh: 约公元550年特奥蒂瓦坎的衰落由哪些问题促成？

options.en: Droughts, crop failures, and an attack that burned the temples
options.zh: 干旱、农作物歉收，以及烧毁神庙的袭击

options.en: A flood that drowned the city

explain.en: 'a period of lengthy droughts, crop failures, and hunger... Then, in 550 CE, outsiders attacked and burned the temples and palaces.'
explain.zh: “一段漫长的干旱、农作物歉收和饥饿……随后在550年，外敌袭击并烧毁了神庙和宫殿。”

q.en: The author describes apartment compounds that housed 'farmers, merchants, and laborers... not just the wealthy.' What does this show?
q.zh: 作者描述容纳“农民、商人、劳工……而非仅富人”的公寓建筑群，显示了什么？

options.en: That ordinary people lived well in Teotihuacán
options.zh: 特奥蒂瓦坎的普通百姓生活得很好

explain.en: Compounds were 'built for everyone... not just the wealthy' with courtyards, fresh water, and drains; 'Ordinary people lived very well in Teotihuacán.'
explain.zh: 建筑群“为所有人而建……而不只是富人”，有庭院、清水和排水沟；“普通百姓生活得很好”。

q.en: Only 5 percent has been excavated and new discoveries keep appearing. What is the author's purpose in ending this way?
q.zh: 仅5%被发掘，新发现不断出现。作者以此结尾的目的是什么？

options.en: To show that the story of Teotihuacán is still being uncovered
options.zh: 表明特奥蒂瓦坎的故事仍在被揭示

options.en: To prove the city is finished being studied
options.zh: 证明这座城市已研究完毕

options.en: To say nothing more will be found

explain.en: The final section notes 'Only 5 percent of the city has been scientifically excavated' and a 2013 robot found a golden chamber, ending on ongoing discovery.
explain.zh: 最后部分指出“全城仅5%被科学发掘”，且2013年机器人发现黄金室，以持续的发现收尾。

prompt.en: Teotihuacán was a planned city where ordinary families lived well, yet much of it remains unexcavated. If you were an archaeologist there, what would you most want to discover?
prompt.zh: 特奥蒂瓦坎是一座规划有序的城市，普通家庭也生活得很好，但大部分仍未发掘。若你是那里的考古学家，你最想发现什么？

en: Name one mystery about the city you find intriguing (its rulers, language, or daily life).
Explain what finding it could teach us about the people who lived there.
zh: 说出你感兴趣的一个城市之谜（统治者、语言或日常生活）。
解释找到它会让我们了解到关于当地居民的什么。
